lines: +summary: "[PROMQL] Bug fix: correctly adjust quantile phi to percentile scale"
+// avg by (cluster) (network.cost) = {staging:5.2917, prod:6.2083, qa:8.8333}; φ=0.5 selects the median (prod).
PROMQL index=k8s step=1h result=(quantile(0.5, avg by (cluster) (network.cost)));
+// PromQL φ is in [0, 1], so φ=0.5 selects the median; the values below are the per-cluster medians
+// of the per-series median-over-time of network.bytes_in.
PROMQL index=k8s step=1h quantile=(round(quantile by (cluster) (0.5, quantile_over_time(0.5, network.bytes_in[1h])), 0.001))
+// PromQL φ is in [0, 1], so φ=1.0 must select the maximum across series.
+// hence quantile(1.0, ...) across those series must equal 972.
+// PromQL φ is in [0, 1], so quantile_over_time(1.0, ...) must equal max_over_time over the range.
+// Therefore sum(quantile_over_time(1.0, network.cost[1m])) must match sum(max_over_time(network.cost[1m]))
+PROMQL index=k8s step=1m start="2024-05-10T00:08:00.000Z" end="2024-05-10T00:10:00.000Z" cost=(sum(quantile_over_time(1.0, network.cost[1m])))
+         * PromQL {@code quantile} and {@code quantile_over_time} take the quantile φ in the range [0, 1], but the
+         * φ value was passed straight through to the ES|QL {@code PERCENTILE} aggregation (which expects [0, 100]),
+         * so e.g. {@code quantile(1.0, x)} returned ≈ the minimum instead of the maximum. φ is now scaled by 100.
* Bugfix in query approximation to not rewrite non-approximable FORK branches:
@@ -49,7 +49,16 @@ public class Percentile extends NumericAggregate implements SurrogateExpression
public static final PromqlFunctionDefinition PROMQL_DEFINITION = PromqlFunctionDefinition.def()
+            (source, field, filter, window, phi) -> new Percentile(
.description("Returns the φ-quantile (0 ≤ φ ≤ 1) of the values across the input vector.")
@@ -35,7 +35,16 @@ public class PercentileOverTime extends TimeSeriesAggregateFunction implements S
public static final PromqlFunctionDefinition PROMQL_DEFINITION = PromqlFunctionDefinition.def()
+            (source, field, filter, window, phi) -> new PercentileOverTime(
.description("Returns the φ-quantile (0 ≤ φ ≤ 1) of the values in the specified time range.")
public static final PromqlParamInfo MIN_SCALAR = PromqlParamInfo.of("min", PromqlDataType.SCALAR, "Minimum value.");
public static final PromqlParamInfo MAX_SCALAR = PromqlParamInfo.of("max", PromqlDataType.SCALAR, "Maximum value.");
+     * Scales a PromQL quantile φ (in the range [0, 1]) to the percentile value (in the range [0, 100]) expected by
+     * the ES|QL {@code PERCENTILE} aggregation that PromQL {@code quantile} and {@code quantile_over_time} translate
+     * into. Without this scaling, e.g. {@code quantile(1.0, x)} would collapse to the 0.01th percentile (≈ the
+     * minimum) instead of returning the maximum.
+    public static Expression quantileToPercentile(Source source, Expression phi) {
* Create a builder for a {@link PromqlFunctionDefinition}.
+     * PromQL {@code quantile} and {@code quantile_over_time} take the quantile φ in the range [0, 1], whereas the
+     * ES|QL {@link Percentile} aggregation they translate into expects a percentile in the range [0, 100]. The
+     * PromQL builders must therefore scale φ by 100. Without this scaling, {@code quantile(1.0, x)} would, for
+     * example, collapse to the 0.01th percentile (≈ the minimum) instead of returning the maximum.
+    private void assertPhiScaledToPercentile(String function, double phi, double expectedPercentile) {
+        var ctx = new PromqlFunctionRegistry.PromqlContext(Literal.NULL, Literal.NULL, Literal.NULL, EsqlTestUtils.TEST_CFG);
+        Percentile percentile = built instanceof PercentileOverTime overTime
assertConstantResult("round(vector(pi()))", equalTo(3.0)); // round down to nearest integer
assertConstantResult("round(vector(pi()), 1)", equalTo(3.0)); // same as above but with explicit argument
